title: The Calzone

jokes.text: I love when the car place puts that paper mat on the floor. Like they're so obsessed with cleanliness. They don't even want their shoes to touch the carpet. The mechanic comes out, he looks like Al Jolson. He's covered in goo from head to toe. You can't even see him.
jokes.setup: Auto shops put down protective paper mats suggesting cleanliness obsession
jokes.punchline: The mechanic emerges completely filthy, negating any cleanliness effort
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor with perfect ironic contrast and visual imagery

jokes.text: I prefer that to when they have the lab coat, the clipboard, the glasses. Now you know you're getting screwed. 'Can I see it?' 'You better not. It's idling quietly right now. I think it should stay overnight. We want to keep an eye on it, and want to keep the bill running up.'
jokes.setup: Mechanics who look professional with lab coats and clipboards
jokes.punchline: The more professional they look, the more they'll overcharge you
jokes.explanation: Solid observational setup with believable mechanic dialogue mimicking their tactics

jokes.text: Tell the players that I reimbursed the trainer... for the cigarettes and shakes. -The players will be reimbursed? -The trainer.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner giving confusing instructions about reimbursement
jokes.punchline: The trainer was reimbursed for cigarettes and shakes, not the players
jokes.explanation: Classic Steinbrenner confusion but relatively straightforward miscommunication

jokes.text: This is the third time I've had to repeat myself. -Sorry. -Sorry doesn't cut it. We're running a ball club here, George. You've got to pay attention.
jokes.setup: George not paying attention to instructions
jokes.punchline: Steinbrenner's over-the-top reaction to a simple mistake
jokes.explanation: Typical Steinbrenner bombast, well-executed character work

jokes.text: Let me-- Let me think here.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner trying to remember George's assignment
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable pause reveals he has no idea what he assigned
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective beat comedy showing Steinbrenner's scattered nature

jokes.text: The Peace Corps gave me my start in this business. Clothing the naked natives of Bangtubesh. The Pygmy pullover.
jokes.setup: Peterman explaining his business origins in the Peace Corps
jokes.punchline: Making clothes for natives, creating the absurd 'Pygmy pullover'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman character moment with invented place name and product

jokes.text: To me, they capture that indefinable romance that was Camelot. -Whatever.
jokes.setup: Peterman waxing poetic about JFK's golf clubs and Camelot
jokes.punchline: Elaine's completely dismissive 'Whatever' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Peterman's grandiosity vs Elaine's bluntness

jokes.text: So he walks out of the stall. He's been talking the whole time. -He pulled an LBJ on you. -LBJ? Lyndon Johnson used to do that to his staffers.
jokes.setup: Someone talking while in a bathroom stall
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to LBJ's infamous bathroom meetings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical reference that creates a perfect verb phrase 'pulled an LBJ'

jokes.text: He'd hold national security meetings in there. He planned the Hanoi bombing after a bad Thai meal.
jokes.setup: LBJ holding meetings in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Planning military operations after digestive issues from Thai food
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic historical speculation with perfect cause-and-effect logic

jokes.text: Ask him to repeat it. Tell him there was an echo. -I can't. He's been on me about not paying attention. It's too late. I already told him I heard him.
jokes.setup: George needs to ask for clarification but can't admit he wasn't listening
jokes.punchline: He's trapped by his own lie about paying attention
jokes.explanation: Classic George self-created predicament with realistic workplace anxiety

jokes.text: Oh, the steering wheel fell off. I don't know where it is.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he couldn't drive his own car
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that the steering wheel fell off and he lost it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer absurdism delivered with complete nonchalance

jokes.text: Well, I'm not paying the 5 cents for that stupid recycling thing. -You don't pay 5 cents. You get 5 cents back.
jokes.setup: Kramer complaining about paying for recycling
jokes.punchline: He completely misunderstands how bottle deposits work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kramer's confusion about basic systems

jokes.text: Well, what do you think the hobos are doing? -I don't know. They're deranged.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about homeless people collecting bottles
jokes.punchline: Kramer's dismissive explanation that they're just crazy
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat but relies on easy target for humor

jokes.text: Wait a minute, you mean you get 5 cents here and 10 cents there? You could round up bottles and run them to Michigan.
jokes.setup: Learning about different deposit values in different states
jokes.punchline: Immediately conceiving an interstate bottle arbitrage scheme
jokes.explanation: Seeds the episode's main plot with Jerry's typical overthinking of minor details

jokes.text: You overload your inventory, and you blow your margins on gasoline.
jokes.setup: Newman explaining why the bottle scheme won't work
jokes.punchline: Using serious business terminology for a ridiculous bottle operation
jokes.explanation: Newman's unexpected business acumen applied to petty schemes

jokes.text: You tried it? -Oh, yeah. Every which way. Couldn't crunch the numbers. It drove me crazy.
jokes.setup: Newman admitting he tried the bottle scheme
jokes.punchline: He's obsessively analyzed it from every angle and failed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman character reveal showing his scheming nature and failures

jokes.text: Sue Ellen Mischke, the braless, Oh Henry! candy bar heiress.
jokes.setup: Identifying someone at the auction
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous description combining personal habits with candy fortune
jokes.explanation: Iconic character description that became a series catchphrase

jokes.text: Come to catch a glimpse of high society? -No, no, I'm actually here to bid, Sue Ellen. I mean, that is if anything's to my liking. -I'm here to catch a glimpse of high society.
jokes.setup: Sue Ellen condescendingly asking if they're there to observe
jokes.punchline: Jerry honestly admits that's exactly why he's there
jokes.explanation: Good reversal where Jerry's honesty deflates the social pretension

jokes.text: No, an 18-wheeler's no good. Too much overhead. You got permits, weigh stations, tolls. Look, you're way out of your league.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of bottle transportation logistics
jokes.punchline: Newman's detailed knowledge of trucking regulations for bottles
jokes.explanation: Continues Newman's unexpected expertise theme but less impactful than setup

jokes.text: ...as seen in the famous photo of the president chipping at Burning Tree on the morning of the Bay of Pigs Invasion.
jokes.setup: Description of JFK's golf clubs with historical context
jokes.punchline: Playing golf on the morning of a major foreign policy disaster
jokes.explanation: Dark historical irony about presidential priorities during crises

jokes.text: Ten thousand to the shapely woman on the left.
jokes.setup: Auctioneer acknowledging bids
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately describing bidders by their physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about auction etiquette but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, you idiots!
jokes.setup: Jerry discovering groceries were stored under his car hood
jokes.punchline: His exasperated reaction to the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: The AAA guy said I was close to sucking a muffin down the carburettor.
jokes.setup: Explaining the car trouble caused by food storage
jokes.punchline: Vivid image of breakfast food being sucked into engine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd visual with perfect technical terminology

jokes.text: I remember this car. Beautiful car.
jokes.setup: Tony the mechanic seeing Jerry's car
jokes.punchline: Setting up his obsessive attachment that will drive the plot
jokes.explanation: Important setup for Tony's character arc but subtle in isolation

jokes.text: Have I been picking at it? No, you know, it's just wear and tear. -Wear and tear, I see.
jokes.setup: Tony asking if Jerry has been picking at the shift knob
jokes.punchline: Jerry's defensive response and Tony's skeptical reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character tension building Jerry's guilt about car maintenance

jokes.text: Fifty-one percent of all turns are right turns.
jokes.setup: Tony explaining tire rotation importance
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific statistic delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony obsessiveness with fake-sounding but believable statistic

jokes.text: Certainly not some stuck-up candy-bar heiress who flaunts herself in public without regard--
jokes.setup: Elaine explaining why she overbid at the auction
jokes.punchline: Getting worked up about Sue Ellen's braless behavior
jokes.explanation: Continues Elaine's rivalry theme but doesn't add much new

jokes.text: So I was just wondering... what do you think would be the very best way to get started?
jokes.setup: George trying to figure out his mystery assignment
jokes.punchline: Asking how to start something he's supposed to already understand
jokes.explanation: Classic George workplace predicament with mounting anxiety

jokes.text: Payroll!
jokes.setup: George finally learning what his assignment involves
jokes.punchline: His realization and panic about the payroll project
jokes.explanation: Decent payoff to the setup but straightforward revelation

jokes.text: He'll tell you what I'm doing here. Then you can tell me.
jokes.setup: George bluffing his way into the payroll office
jokes.punchline: Hoping Wilhelm will explain the project to someone who can explain it to him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George logic - using his ignorance as a weapon in the bluff

jokes.text: Look, you were right, I was wrong. You don't have to humiliate me about it.
jokes.setup: George asking what Wilhelm told the payroll guy
jokes.punchline: The guy assumes George is gloating instead of genuinely confused
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding comedy but not exceptional

jokes.text: Wait a second. Mother's Day.
jokes.setup: Newman realizing the mail truck opportunity
jokes.punchline: The holiday that will provide the transportation they need
jokes.explanation: Important plot revelation that enables the scheme

jokes.text: The mother of all mail days.
jokes.setup: Explaining Mother's Day mail volume
jokes.punchline: Pun connecting 'mother' holiday with 'mother of all' expression
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but somewhat predictable wordplay

jokes.text: Newman, you magnificent bastard, you did it!
jokes.setup: Kramer praising Newman's scheme solution
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top military movie reference for bottle scheme
jokes.explanation: Good movie reference but somewhat obvious choice for the situation

jokes.text: There's your answer, 'Downtown.' The song 'Downtown'? The Petula Clark song?
jokes.setup: Wilhelm giving cryptic advice about George's project
jokes.punchline: His only guidance is referencing a 1960s pop song
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Wilhelm moment using pop culture as work guidance

jokes.text: Little places to go that never close. What little place never closes? -7-Eleven. -Maybe.
jokes.setup: Analyzing song lyrics for work clues
jokes.punchline: Treating convenience stores as potential job locations
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic treating song lyrics as work instructions

jokes.text: Well, don't hang around and let your troubles surround you.
jokes.setup: George despairing about his situation
jokes.punchline: Jerry quoting more song lyrics as advice
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but not adding much beyond the original bit

jokes.text: Motor oil is the lifeblood of a car. You put in a low-grade oil, you can damage vital engine parts.
jokes.setup: Tony discovering Jerry uses quick-lube oil
jokes.punchline: Treating oil choice as a life-or-death automotive decision
jokes.explanation: Good character obsessiveness but fairly predictable mechanic behavior

jokes.text: The washer fluid is not fine!
jokes.setup: Jerry insisting his washer fluid is adequate
jokes.punchline: Tony's emphatic disagreement about minor maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tony's obsessive character but straightforward

jokes.text: Well, I didn't count on my mechanic pulling a Mary Beth Whitehead, did I?
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he left clubs in the car
jokes.punchline: Comparing mechanic to famous custody battle figure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant topical reference comparing car theft to custody disputes

jokes.text: The mechanic forms an emotional attachment... thinks he's gonna lose the car. He panics and does something rash.
jokes.setup: Detective explaining the car theft pattern
jokes.punchline: Treating mechanic attachment like a psychological condition
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on car theft as relationship psychology

jokes.text: What does it matter? It's my car. I can do whatever I want with it. Not that I would think of doing such things.
jokes.setup: Detective asking about Jerry's car care habits
jokes.punchline: Jerry defending his rights then immediately backtracking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's guilt and defensiveness

jokes.text: Jimmy crack corn and I don't care.
jokes.setup: George describing his mysterious work success
jokes.punchline: Using old folk song to express indifference
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to song theme but not particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: The gardener did a nice job planting the rose bushes, didn't he, dear? -You planted the rose bushes, dear. -I did? -Yesterday. You remember. -That's right.
jokes.setup: Elderly man complimenting gardening work
jokes.punchline: He doesn't remember doing the work himself
jokes.explanation: Mild dementia humor but not particularly well-developed

jokes.text: 9999 bottles and cans in the truck... At $0.10 a bottle and $0.10 a can We're pulling in $500 a man
jokes.setup: Singing while collecting bottles
jokes.punchline: Adapting '99 bottles of beer' song to their scheme
jokes.explanation: Creative song adaptation but goes on too long for the payoff

jokes.text: That registration may have your name on it, Jerry... but this engine's running on my sweat and my blood.
jokes.setup: Jerry demanding his car back
jokes.punchline: Tony claiming ownership through emotional investment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tony escalation showing his delusional attachment to the car

jokes.text: We're right on this guy like stink on a monkey.
jokes.setup: Kramer reporting they're following Tony
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'stink on shit' with monkey reference
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer malapropism with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Bottles below!
jokes.setup: Newman dumping bottles from the truck
jokes.punchline: Military-style warning for falling recyclables
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with appropriate Newman dramatics

jokes.text: My brother-in-law was there. Man was obsessed with lactating women. They cured him. Although he eats a lot of cheese.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner reassuring George about mental health treatment
jokes.punchline: Bizarre example of treatment success with disturbing side effect
jokes.explanation: Peak Steinbrenner absurdism with perfect inappropriate overshare and logical connection

jokes.text: I didn't do it! It just got done! I don't know how it got done but it did!
jokes.setup: George being dragged away for mental health treatment
jokes.punchline: His increasingly frantic explanation making him sound more insane
jokes.explanation: Perfect George spiral where the truth sounds like delusion

jokes.text: I just have one rule. Keep your hands off my daughter.
jokes.setup: Farmer offering hospitality to stranded Newman
jokes.punchline: Classic overprotective father warning
jokes.explanation: Very predictable setup for the daughter subplot, minimal originality

jokes.text: You'll have to do a lot better than that!
jokes.setup: Tony throwing golf clubs at the truck
jokes.punchline: Kramer taunting during the highway chase
jokes.explanation: Good action comedy moment but fairly standard chase scene banter

jokes.text: No, Daddy, you'll hurt him. I love him. Goodbye, Norman! Goodbye!
jokes.setup: Newman being chased by the farmer
jokes.punchline: Daughter's dramatic declaration of love for Newman
jokes.explanation: Decent romantic comedy parody but somewhat predictable outcome

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. The only thing worse was his slice.
jokes.setup: Peterman surprised by JFK's temper based on damaged clubs
jokes.punchline: Elaine blaming Kennedy's golf swing for the damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending joke with deadpan delivery covering up the chase damage

tmdb_overview: George gets his boss, George Steinbrenner, hooked on calzones, then loses his supplier; Kramer warms to clothing fresh from the dryer.
